entries.final_answer: The Standards & Security report's PWA checks (manifest, service worker) are presence-only booleans with no evidence, mixed into a flat check list. Issue #145 asks for a dedicated Progressive Web Resilience section reporting manifest characteristics, service-worker state, offline/network resilience, and installability as Pass/Fail/N/A with evidence — not another aggregate score.
entries.question: What problem does this feature solve?
entries.rationale: Derived from spec.md Problem/Goal sections, authored from issue #145 and a read of src/engines/standards.js.

entries.final_answer: Extend src/engines/standards.js's existing page.evaluate block to fetch/parse the manifest and expand service-worker state (IC-01, IC-02), add an origin-level memoized offline/network-resilience check in src/scan.js mirroring the existing runSecurity/runPublicInterest pattern with an isolated browser context (IC-03), derive installability from those outputs (IC-04), and restructure runStandards()'s return shape into a new resilience section migrated from the flat checks array, rendered as a distinct report subsection and threaded through the CSV/JSON API pipeline (IC-05).
entries.question: What is the high-level implementation approach?
entries.rationale: Derived from plan.md's Implementation Concern Map (IC-01..IC-05), grounded in research.md's D-01..D-06 decisions about scan.js's per-page vs per-origin execution model.
